channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: When
item.description: Our procurement team was reconciling a batch of vendor invoices against purchase orders. They used a matching tool that returned two categories: Match and No Match. Simple. Binary. Clean. The...

item.title: We Scanned 200 SMB Domains. Here's What We Found.
item.description: We Scanned 200 SMB Domains. Here's What We Found. Published by the ComplianceLayer team | March 2026 Last quarter, we ran ComplianceLayer against 200 small and medium business domains — companies...

item.title: Benchmarking Memoria on LongMemEval: Strong Memory Retrieval, Clear Reader Separation
item.description: Memory systems for AI agents are easy to demo and much harder to evaluate. A good anecdote can make any memory layer look impressive. A real benchmark has to answer a tougher question: when an...

item.title: Stop Googling 'Can I Use That AI Feature on the Free Plan'
item.description: Someone raises their hand: "Can I actually use that on the free plan?" You're 90% sure the answer is yes. But the vendor changed their pricing page last month. And the feature you demoed in...

item.title: Most LCP Fixes Come Down to One Image
item.description: A Next.js app with next/image on every image component. Lighthouse image audit: no issues. LCP: 4.2 seconds. The hero was a CSS background-image. next/image doesn't touch those. Nobody had checked...

item.title: Ronan Farrow on Sam Altman’s ‘unconstrained’ relationship with the truth
item.description: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Ronan Farrow, one of the biggest stars of investigative reporting working today. He broke the Harvey Weinstein story, among many, many others. And just last...

item.title: Character.AI’s new Books mode turns reading into roleplay
item.description: Mired in controversy and legal woes over concerns about its chatbots' interactions with users, particularly teens, Character.AI seems to be playing it safer with a new "Books" mode. The new format...

item.title: The Cybertruck of e-bikes is here to replace your car
item.description: It was at about 36 miles per hour that I decided the Infinite Machine Olto is not a bike. Sure, it has pedals, you don't need a license to ride it in most (but not all!) places in the US, and the...

item.title: CodeClone b5: structural review that finally knows what your tests cover
item.description: In earlier posts, I wrote about why I built CodeClone, why I exposed it through MCP for AI agents, and how b4 turned it into a real review surface for VS Code, Claude Desktop, and Codex. b5 is the...

item.title: System Design: проектируем сервис заказа такси

item.title: AI Doesn't Fix Weak Engineering. It Just Speeds It Up.
item.description: "Weak engineers with AI still produce weak output. Just faster." That was the whole point. AI changes speed. Not judgment. If your team already struggled to make sound architectural decisions, the...

item.title: The Setup Is the Strategy: How I Orchestrated a Product Migration with Claude Code
item.description: Most engineers using Claude Code are getting a fraction of its value. Not because the tool isn't capable — but because they're using it out of the box, unconfigured, the way you'd use a new IDE...

item.title: What UK Businesses Get Wrong About GDPR and Phone Calls
item.description: Most UK businesses think GDPR applies to emails and websites. They forget that phone calls generate personal data too — and the compliance gaps are enormous. After auditing GDPR telephony...

item.title: Anthropic Plots Major London Expansion
item.description: As tensions with the US government mount, Anthropic has leased a new office with enough space to quadruple its 200-person headcount in London.

item.title: Meta raises Quest 3 and Quest 3S prices due to RAM shortage
item.description: Starting April 19, the price of the Meta Quest 3S (128GB) and Meta Quest 3S (256GB) will go up by $50 to $349.99 and $449.99, respectively. The price of the Meta Quest 3 is going up by $100 to...

item.title: Moft adds a tracker and shutter button to its magnetic tripod wallet
item.description: Moft's MagSafe wallets have long been overachievers with folding designs that double as smartphone stands. Its latest creation, the Moft Trackable Tripod Wallet, does even more. It carries forward...

item.title: Canva’s AI 2.0 update goes all in on prompt-powered design tools
item.description: Canva has overhauled its design and workspace suite as it attempts to become the ultimate centralized hub for AI-powered content creation. The platform announced its Canva AI 2.0 update today,...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
item.description: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you...

item.title: Canva’s AI assistant can now call various tools to make designs for you
item.description: The latest version of Canva's AI assistant lets users create editable designs with text prompts.

item.title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
item.description: The AI boom has hit across industries, and public sector organizations are facing pressure to accelerate adoption. At the same time, government institutions face distinct constraints around...

item.title: Treating enterprise AI as an operating layer
item.description: There’s a fault line running through enterprise AI, and it’s not the one getting the most attention. The public conversation still tracks foundation models and benchmarks — GPT versus Gemini,...

item.title: Meta blames RAM shortage for $100 Quest 3 price hike
item.description: Meta is the next tech company to hike up hardware prices due to the global memory shortage. Beginning April 19th, Meta's 128GB Quest 3S VR headset will cost $349.99, the 256GB Quest 3S will cost...

item.title: Intel’s cheaper Panther Lake chips are for budget-friendly laptops
item.description: Intel is announcing a family of cheaper Panther Lake laptop processors called the Core Series 3 line. They're built on the same Intel 18A process as the higher-end Core Ultra Series 3 family of...

item.title: This simulation startup wants to be the Cursor for physical AI
item.description: Antioch raised a $8.5 million seed round to create simulation tools for a new generation of robot builders.

item.title: The Download: cyberscammers’ banking bypasses, and carbon removal troubles
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Cyberscammers are bypassing banks’ security with illicit...

item.title: Why having “humans in the loop” in an AI war is an illusion
item.description: The availability of artificial intelligence for use in warfare is at the center of a legal battle between Anthropic and the Pentagon. This debate has become urgent, with AI playing a bigger role...

item.title: DJI’s Osmo Pocket 4 camera is better at capturing slo-mo footage and photos
item.description: Following months of leaks, DJI has announced the fourth version of its handheld stabilized vlogger camera. Unlike the Osmo Pocket 3 that debuted way back in September 2023 with major upgrades like...

item.title: Govee’s new LED Lightwall comes with its own self-standing frame
item.description: Govee has announced an upgraded version of its hanging Curtain Lights Pro that can instead be used nearly anywhere you have access to an outlet or large battery. At $449.99, Govee's new Lightwall...

item.title: [Webinar] Find and Eliminate Orphaned Non-Human Identities in Your Environment
item.description: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching. For every...

item.title: Spotify just won $322 million from music pirates it can’t find
item.description: Spotify and the three major labels have won a $322 million default judgement against Anna's Archive, the open-source library and pirate activist group that planned to publicly release millions of...
